questions.question: How do you create a CSS triangle using border tricks?
choices: Set clip-path to a triangle polygon
Set width and height to 0, then use colored and transparent borders
Apply transform: rotate(45deg) to a square element
questions.explanation: A CSS triangle is created by setting width and height to 0, making all borders transparent, and then giving one border a color. The colored border forms the visible triangle shape.

questions.question: Which CSS property creates a perfect circle from a square element?
questions.explanation: Setting border-radius: 50% on a square element (equal width and height) produces a perfect circle. The percentage is relative to the element's dimensions.

questions.question: Which pseudo-elements are commonly used to add extra shape components to a single HTML element?
questions.explanation: The ::before and ::after pseudo-elements create additional renderable boxes within a single element, giving you up to three boxes (the element plus two pseudo-elements) to compose complex shapes.

questions.question: When creating a heart shape with CSS, which technique combines two rotated shapes?
choices: Using two overlapping circles and a rotated square
Using a single gradient with two color stops
Applying border-radius to all four corners differently
Using two box-shadows at offset positions
questions.explanation: A CSS heart shape is typically created by combining two circles (using border-radius) with a rotated square. The ::before and ::after pseudo-elements create the rounded tops while the rotated parent forms the point.

questions.question: To create a triangle pointing downward, set the ___-border to a solid color and all other borders to transparent.
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is formed by coloring only the top border while making the left, right, and bottom borders transparent. The visible top border creates the triangle pointing down.

questions.question: The ___ property must be set (usually to empty string) on ::before and ::after pseudo-elements for them to render.
questions.explanation: Pseudo-elements ::before and ::after require the content property to be set (even to an empty string '') in order to be generated and rendered in the document.
